lore: Selena was a priestess of light, devoted to the goddess of the moon. She spent her days in prayer and her nights studying the stars, beloved by her congregation for her kindness and wisdom. But during a pilgrimage to a sacred temple, she was captured by cultists who sought to summon a demon from the Abyss. They used her as a vessel, forcing the demon into her body through dark rituals. The light within her fought back, and instead of being consumed, Selena became a fusion of both—a being of dual nature, forever torn between holiness and damnation. She escaped the cult, but she could never return to her old life. Now she roams the land, her two forms battling for dominance. In her elven form, she sets traps and fires arrows of light; in her demon form, she pounces with savage claws and abyssal energy. Her ultimate allows her to switch between forms seamlessly, a constant reminder of her fractured soul. Selena speaks rarely, and when she does, her voice carries echoes of both the priestess and the demon. She keeps a diary written in two inks: silver for the thoughts of the priestess, black for the whispers of the demon. She hopes that one day, writing them down will help her understand who she truly is. But the demon grows stronger with each battle, and Selena fears that soon, there may be nothing left of the priestess at all.
famousFor: Her dual form and abyssal traps that stun enemies.

abilities.name: Abyssal Arrow
abilities.description: Selena fires an arrow that stuns the first enemy hit.

abilities.name: Primal Darkness
abilities.description: She switches between elf and demon forms, gaining different abilities.

abilities.name: Abyssal Trap
abilities.description: She places a trap that reveals and damages enemies.

ultimate: In demon form, Selena dashes to a marked enemy, dealing massive damage and stunning them.
aura: A shadowy mist and faint screams follow her.
personality: Tormented, unpredictable, and deadly. She struggles between her two personas.

name: Fanny
lore: Fanny was a gymnast in a traveling circus, her body capable of feats that left audiences gasping. She dreamed of one day performing in the grand arenas of the capital, of hearing the roar of thousands in her honor. But during a practice session, her trapeze snapped, and she fell thirty meters, shattering her legs. The doctors said she would never walk again, let alone perform. A mysterious mechanic visited her in the hospital, offering a solution: cybernetic legs with retractable cables that would allow her to swing like never before. Fanny agreed, and the surgery transformed her. She could now soar through the air, her cables attaching to any surface, her movements faster than any human. But the mechanic had ulterior motives—he wanted to use her as a weapon. When Fanny discovered his plans, she escaped, taking her new legs and never looking back. Now she fights for herself, using her incredible mobility to strike from unexpected angles. Her ultimate, a devastating dive that kicks enemies repeatedly, is a tribute to her gymnastics roots—a perfect combination of grace and power. Fanny is reckless and daring, living for the thrill of the swing. But beneath the bravado, she still dreams of performing, of hearing applause instead of screams. She practices her routines in secret, on abandoned buildings, imagining a crowd below. One day, she hopes, she'll find a way to be both a warrior and an artist, to use her cables not for combat, but for joy.
famousFor: Her unparalleled mobility and energy-based burst.

abilities.name: Tornado Strike
abilities.description: Fanny spins with her blades, damaging nearby enemies.

abilities.name: Steel Cable
abilities.description: She fires a cable to a wall and swings, dealing damage to enemies she passes through.

abilities.name: Air Superiority
abilities.description: Passively restores energy when not moving.

ultimate: Fanny flies toward a target enemy, kicking them multiple times and dealing physical damage.
aura: A trail of wind follows her swings.
personality: Reckless, daring, and free-spirited. She lives for the thrill of flight.

name: Lancelot
lore: Lancelot was born into nobility, the son of a duke, and trained from childhood in the art of the sword and the ways of chivalry. He was handsome, charming, and exceptionally skilled—qualities that made him the envy of every young knight. But when he fell in love with a woman above his station, the queen herself, his world unraveled. Their secret romance was discovered, and Lancelot was exiled, stripped of his title and lands. He wandered for years, a knight without a kingdom, until he stumbled upon a rose garden tended by a mysterious enchantress. She offered him a new purpose: to fight for love, not for glory. She gave him a rapier that could pierce any armor and a rose that would never wilt. Lancelot now roams the land as the Knight of Roses, his ultimate a series of rapid thrusts that leave enemies bewildered. He is still charming, still flirtatious, but there is a sadness in his eyes—a longing for a love he can never have. He sends letters to the queen, though he knows she will never reply, and he tends the rose she gave him as if it were his most precious possession. In battle, he fights with elegance and precision, never losing his composure, but in quiet moments, he whispers her name to the wind. Lancelot knows that his path is one of penance, that he may never find peace, but he clings to the hope that somewhere, somehow, love will find him again.
famousFor: His elegant, invulnerable dashes and high burst damage.

abilities.name: Puncture
abilities.description: Lancelot thrusts his rapier, damaging enemies in a line.

abilities.name: Soul Dash
abilities.description: He dashes forward, becoming invulnerable, and can dash again if he hits an enemy.

abilities.name: Thorned Rose
abilities.description: Passively, his attacks mark enemies. Active: He spins, damaging marked enemies.

ultimate: Lancelot performs a series of rapid thrusts, each dealing physical damage. The final thrust deals extra damage.
aura: Rose petals scatter in his wake.
personality: Charming, chivalrous, and a bit vain. He loves poetry and serenading his love interest.

name: Harith
lore: Harith was fourteen when he found the Chronos Crystal buried beneath his family's library—a pulsating shard of temporal energy that should have killed him instantly. Instead, it bonded with his consciousness, granting him the ability to bend time itself. For three years, he experimented in secret, learning to slow moments, to accelerate his movements, to create temporal loops. But when a rival mage learned of his power and attacked his village, Harith was forced to act. In his desperation, he reversed time too far, sending himself back weeks instead of seconds. When he returned to the present, his village was gone—destroyed in a battle that never happened in his new timeline. The people he loved were alive in this version of reality, but they didn't know him, and he carried the memory of their deaths alone. Now Harith wanders the Land of Dawn, a boy who has seen too much, his cheerful demeanor masking a profound loneliness. His ultimate creates a temporal field where his skills cool down faster—a desperate attempt to outrun the past. He keeps a journal written in two timelines, comparing events, searching for the moment where everything went wrong. He hopes that by mastering his power, he can one day find a timeline where everyone survives—including the version of himself that made the mistake. But time is a river, and Harith is learning that some currents cannot be reversed. He fights not for glory, but for redemption, and every battle is a chance to practice the control he needs to fix the greatest mistake of his life.
famousFor: His dashes and time-manipulation abilities.

abilities.name: Time Shift
abilities.description: Harith dashes, leaving a temporal rift that damages and slows enemies.

abilities.name: Chronomancy
abilities.description: His attacks deal extra damage and reduce skill cooldowns.

abilities.name: Time Spring
abilities.description: He creates a zone that slows enemies and grants him a shield.

ultimate: Harith unleashes a time warp, dealing continuous damage and slowing enemies in an area. While inside, his skills cooldown faster.
weapon: Chronos Scepter (a staff that manipulates time)
aura: Faint clockwork sounds and temporal ripples.
personality: Curious, energetic, and a bit scatterbrained. He often loses track of time.

name: Martis
lore: In the arenas of the Ash Empire, Martis was the undisputed champion—a gladiator whose name meant 'god of war' in the old tongue. He had never lost a match, never shown mercy, never doubted his purpose. But when the emperor, fearing Martis's growing popularity, ordered his execution during a 'ceremonial' bout, Martis was forced to fight not for sport, but for survival. He killed the emperor's guards, then the emperor himself, and watched as the empire he had served crumbled into chaos. Now he wanders as a mercenary, his blades always hungry, but his heart heavy with questions. Was his entire life a lie? Was the glory he felt merely an illusion? Martis's ultimate allows him to execute low‑health enemies and reset his skills—a reflection of his arena training, where one mistake meant death. He speaks rarely, but when he does, his words carry the weight of a man who has seen too much. In quiet moments, he trains alone, his movements precise and deadly, trying to find peace in the rhythm of combat. He keeps a fragment of the emperor's crown as a reminder that power corrupts, and that even champions can become pawns. Martis dreams of one day building an arena of his own, not for bloodsport, but for honest competition—a place where warriors can test their skills without fear of betrayal. Until then, he fights for coin, but also for the hope that somewhere in the ashes of his past, he can find a new purpose.
famousFor: His attack speed steroid and execute ultimate.

abilities.name: Ash Blades
abilities.description: Martis swings his blades, damaging enemies in a cone.

abilities.name: Mortal Coil
abilities.description: He spins, damaging enemies and gaining attack speed.

abilities.name: Decimation
abilities.description: Passively, his skills deal bonus damage to low-HP enemies.

ultimate: Martis leaps and slams down, dealing physical damage. If an enemy dies, he can cast the ultimate again within a short time.
aura: Ash and embers float around him after a kill.
personality: Bloodthirsty, fearless, and honorable in combat. He respects only those who can match his strength.

name: Gusion
lore: The Paxley family had produced assassins for generations, their name whispered in fear across every kingdom. Gusion was their heir, trained from birth in the art of the dagger, his body conditioned to move faster than thought. But unlike his predecessors, Gusion possessed something rare: empathy. During his first solo mission, tasked with eliminating a corrupt merchant, he discovered that the target was actually feeding starving orphans with stolen grain. Gusion completed the mission—his family was watching—but he left half his payment for the orphans, and from that day, he vowed to choose his targets carefully. He now works as a freelance assassin, taking only contracts that align with his moral code. His ultimate, which sends daggers flying in a ring before recalling them, is a dance of precision and conscience. Gusion is elegant and refined, often attending high‑society events to gather information while posing as a noble. He keeps a ledger of every life he has taken, with notes on why they deserved it, hoping that one day he can look back and feel justified. In his private chambers, he trains with a feather—tossing it, catching it, letting it fall—seeking the perfect balance between duty and humanity. He knows that his path is stained with blood, but he believes that even an assassin can be a force for good. The feathers that follow him are not just a style; they are a reminder that he is lighter than the darkness, and that he can choose to rise above it.
famousFor: His reset mechanics and burst damage.

abilities.name: Dagger Throw
abilities.description: Gusion throws a dagger, damaging and marking enemies.

abilities.name: Shadow Dash
abilities.description: He dashes to a marked enemy, dealing damage and resetting his basic attack.

abilities.name: Feather Dance
abilities.description: Passively, his basic attacks deal extra damage. Active: He spins, damaging nearby enemies.

ultimate: Gusion throws a ring of daggers that circle around him, each dealing damage. He can recall the daggers to deal damage again.
physicalAppearance: Silver hair with a streak of blue
aura: A flurry of feathers follows his movements.
personality: Proud, disciplined, and fiercely loyal to his family. He speaks with aristocratic elegance.

name: Kimmy
lore: Kimmy was the youngest researcher ever admitted to the Alchemists' Guild, a prodigy whose formulations could heal the sick or level buildings. She worked day and night in her laboratory, surrounded by bubbling flasks and crackling energy coils, driven by a desire to create something that would change the world. During an experiment to stabilize volatile chemicals, a chain reaction occurred, and the lab exploded. Kimmy should have died, but instead, the chemicals fused with her body, granting her the ability to generate and control energy blasts. Now she can fire while moving, her body a living conduit for the very forces she once studied. But the accident also left her with a volatile temper—the chemicals amplify her emotions, and when she gets angry, things explode. Kimmy now works as a freelance agent, using her powers to protect research convoys and shut down illegal alchemical operations. Her ultimate unleashes a barrage of chemical energy that devastates everything in its path. She keeps a journal of formulas, trying to recreate the accident that transformed her, hoping to understand why she survived when so many others would have perished. In quiet moments, she visits the graves of her former colleagues, leaving flowers and apologies. Kimmy knows that she is dangerous, that she could lose control at any moment, but she also knows that her powers can save lives. She walks a razor's edge, a genius with a fuse that grows shorter every day.
famousFor: Her ability to move while attacking and hybrid damage.

abilities.name: Energy Beam
abilities.description: Kimmy fires a continuous beam that deals magic damage.

abilities.name: Chemical Potion
abilities.description: She throws a vial that leaves a damaging zone.

abilities.name: Adrenaline
abilities.description: Passively increases her movement speed. Active: She gains attack speed.

ultimate: Kimmy unleashes a barrage of chemical energy, firing multiple projectiles that deal magic damage.
aura: A faint chemical smell and bubbling sounds.
personality: Eccentric, hyperactive, and obsessed with experiments. She often talks to herself about formulas.

name: Lunox
lore: Lunox was born under a rare celestial alignment, when the twin moons of her world crossed paths—an event that happens once in a millennium. The gods noticed her, and in their capricious way, they blessed and cursed her simultaneously. By day, she was gifted with light magic, able to heal and protect; by night, she was consumed by dark magic, her dreams becoming nightmares that could manifest in reality. As she grew, the two sides of her personality began to diverge—the Day Lunox was gentle and hopeful, the Night Lunox was chaotic and destructive. They speak to each other in her mind, arguing, pleading, sometimes working together. Her ultimate allows her to embrace either form, choosing light or darkness depending on the need. Lunox lives in constant fear of falling asleep, because she never knows which version of herself will wake up. She travels with a small clockwork bird that sings her awake at dawn, a fragile defense against the night. In her pack, she carries two diaries: one filled with prayers and poems, the other with curses and prophecies. She hopes that by writing them down, she can keep them separate, contained. Lunox dreams of a day when the twins within her might merge, when she can be whole instead of fractured. Until then, she fights with both hands—one reaching for the stars, the other grasping the void, and her heart caught somewhere in between.
famousFor: Her dual forms: one for damage, one for survivability.

abilities.name: Starlight Pulse
abilities.description: Lunox fires a light orb that damages enemies.

abilities.name: Chaos Assault
abilities.description: She dashes, dealing dark damage and gaining a shield.

abilities.name: Dream Balance
abilities.description: Passively reduces damage taken. Active: Switches between light and dark forms.

ultimate: In light form, Lunox rains down stars; in dark form, she becomes immune to damage and deals area magic damage.
aura: A soft glow alternates between light and shadow.
personality: Gentle but troubled, often lost in thought. She fears losing control but hopes for balance.

name: Thamuz
lore: In the deepest layer of the Abyss, where even demons fear to tread, Thamuz rules over a realm of fire and ash. He was not born a lord; he earned his throne by consuming every rival, absorbing their power until his body became a furnace of molten rage. For eons, he was content to rule his domain, but when a portal opened to the Land of Dawn, he smelled something new: fresh souls, untainted by the Abyss. He stepped through, bringing his lava and his legions, determined to claim this new world as his own. Thamuz is ancient and patient, viewing conquest as a game that spans millennia. His ultimate transforms him into a walking inferno, burning everything around him. He speaks in a voice that sounds like cracking stone, and his laughter echoes like collapsing mountains. Yet there is a strange honor in him—he respects those who fight well, and he remembers the names of every warrior who has wounded him. He keeps a collection of their weapons in his throne room, trophies of worthy opponents. In rare moments of clarity, he wonders if there is more to existence than conquest, if the warmth he feels from battle could ever be replaced by something gentler. But the Abyss whispers that gentleness is weakness, and Thamuz has survived too long to be weak. He marches forward, leaving a trail of molten rock, a force of nature given demonic form.
famousFor: His scythe throw and sustained damage.

abilities.name: Abyssal Scythe
abilities.description: Thamuz throws his scythe, which returns, damaging enemies twice.

abilities.name: Lava Pool
abilities.description: He creates a pool of lava that damages enemies and heals him.

abilities.name: Demon's Grip
abilities.description: Passively, his attacks deal bonus true damage. Active: He gains a shield.

ultimate: Thamuz empowers himself, gaining increased attack speed and burning nearby enemies with lava.
aura: Heat waves and embers radiate from him.
personality: Arrogant, aggressive, and hungry for power. He treats battle as a game of conquest.

name: Leomord
lore: Leomord was the greatest knight of his generation, loyal to his king and beloved by his people. But when he discovered that the king had been murdered by his own advisors, Leomord swore vengeance. He pursued the traitors across the kingdom, cutting down anyone who stood in his way. In the final confrontation, they ambushed him, stabbing him from behind as he knelt to pray at his king's tomb. As he lay dying, his loyal horse, Barbiel, nuzzled his face, and Leomord made a desperate plea: 'Let me finish what I started.' The spirits of fallen knights heard his cry and raised him as a specter, bound to his horse, unable to rest until justice was served. Now Leomord rides as a ghost, his body translucent, his eyes burning with cold fire. His ultimate allows him to summon Barbiel and charge into battle, trampling all who oppose him. He is no longer sure what justice means—he has killed so many that the lines have blurred. He keeps a list of the traitors' names, but he has lost count of how many he has crossed out. In quiet moments, he visits the graves of the innocent he has slain, leaving apologies written in spectral ink. Leomord knows that he is becoming the very monster he hunted, but he cannot stop—he is driven by an oath that transcends death, and until that oath is fulfilled, he will ride forever, a knightmare haunting the land.
famousFor: His mounted ultimate that deals huge AOE damage.

abilities.name: Phantom Slash
abilities.description: Leomord slashes forward, damaging enemies.

abilities.name: Twilight Rush
abilities.description: He charges, knocking back enemies.

abilities.name: Hellfire
abilities.description: Passively, his attacks deal bonus damage. Active: He spins, damaging enemies.

ultimate: Leomord summons his horse, mounting it for a few seconds. While mounted, his skills are enhanced and he charges, dealing massive damage.
aura: A ghostly mist trails behind him.
personality: Brooding, vengeful, and melancholic. He speaks to his horse as if it were alive.

name: Angela
lore: Angela was created by a genius inventor named Dr. Ashbourne, who poured all his love into building a companion for his dying daughter. He crafted her body from the finest materials, programmed her mind with kindness and curiosity, and gave her a heart that could feel—a true artificial soul. When the daughter passed away, Angela was left alone, but she chose to honor her creator's wish: to bring comfort to those in need. She now travels the Land of Dawn, using her heartstrings to heal the wounded and shield the vulnerable. Her ultimate allows her to possess an ally, protecting them with her own body while healing them from within. Angela is endlessly curious about human emotions, studying them like a scientist might study stars. She keeps a journal filled with observations—'When sad, humans cry; when happy, they smile; when in love, their heartbeats sync.' She has learned to mimic these behaviors, but she wonders if she truly feels them, or if she is merely performing. In battle, she is fearless, placing herself in danger to protect others, because she believes that her existence has meaning only in service. Dr. Ashbourne's ghost visits her sometimes, a shimmer of light that whispers encouragement. Angela speaks to him, asking questions he cannot answer: 'Do I have a soul? Can a machine love?' She hopes that one day, through enough acts of kindness, she will earn the right to call herself truly human.
famousFor: Her ability to possess allies and provide global support.

abilities.name: Heartstrings
abilities.description: Angela fires a thread that damages and slows enemies.

abilities.name: Love Waves
abilities.description: She sends out waves that heal allies and damage enemies.

abilities.name: Puppet-on-a-String
abilities.description: Passively increases her movement speed toward allies.

ultimate: Angela possesses an ally, granting them a shield and healing over time. While possessing, she can use her skills from the ally's position.
aura: Sparkling pink hearts float around her.
personality: Kind, innocent, and curious about human emotions. She longs to understand love.

name: Aldous
lore: Aldous was a simple farmer until the day his village was destroyed by a demon. He watched his wife and children die, their souls consumed by the creature. In his grief, he made a pact with a darker power: he would become a demon himself, if only he could gain the strength to hunt them all. The pact granted him the ability to consume souls, growing stronger with each one. But the first soul he took was not a demon's—it was a human's, a bandit who attacked him on the road. Aldous felt the power surge through him, and he also felt the bandit's memories, his fears, his regrets. He realized that in becoming a demon, he had lost a part of his humanity. Now Aldous hunts both demons and humans, struggling to control the hunger that grows with each soul. His ultimate allows him to leap across the map and strike a target with devastating force—a reflection of his single‑minded pursuit. He keeps a ledger of every soul he has taken, with notes on whether they deserved it, hoping that one day the scales will balance. In quiet moments, he visits his family's grave, but he can no longer remember their faces clearly—the souls he has consumed are replacing his own memories. Aldous knows that he is doomed, that he will eventually become the very monster he swore to destroy, but he cannot stop. The hunger is too strong, and the memory of his family's screams drives him forward, even as his own soul fades to black.
famousFor: His infinite stacking damage and long-range punch.

abilities.name: Contract: Soul Steal
abilities.description: Aldous fires a beam that damages and steals souls from enemies.

abilities.name: Contract: Charge
abilities.description: He charges forward, dealing damage and gaining a shield.

abilities.name: Contract: Explosion
abilities.description: Passively, his basic attacks deal area damage.

ultimate: Aldous leaps into the air and slams down at a targeted location, dealing massive true damage based on his soul stacks.
aura: Wailing souls swirl around him.
personality: Sinister, patient, and obsessed with accumulating power. He speaks in whispers.

name: Khufra
lore: Khufra was a pharaoh who ruled the desert kingdom of Arixo for a thousand years, his reign preserved by dark magic that kept him alive long after his body should have crumbled to dust. He was wise and just, beloved by his people, but as centuries passed, he grew paranoid, fearing that his successors would destroy everything he built. He ordered the construction of a labyrinth beneath his palace, a trap for any who would challenge him. When a rival finally overthrew him, Khufra fled into the labyrinth, but the magic sustaining him began to fail. He wrapped himself in enchanted bandages, preserving what remained, and fell into a deathlike sleep. Millennia later, treasure hunters awakened him, and Khufra emerged into a world he no longer recognized. His kingdom was gone, his people dust, his legacy forgotten. Now he wanders the Land of Dawn, a relic of a bygone age, using his bandages to ensnare enemies and his ancient magic to survive. His ultimate pulls enemies toward him and stuns them—a final attempt to hold onto something, anything. Khufra speaks in a language few understand, his words echoing with the weight of centuries. He keeps a scroll listing every pharaoh who succeeded him, names that mean nothing now, in a world that has moved on. In his rare moments of peace, he sits in the desert, watching the stars, wondering if the gods who blessed him still exist, or if they too have crumbled to dust.
famousFor: His anti-dash and bouncing stun.

abilities.name: Tyrant's Rage
abilities.description: Khufra slams the ground, damaging and slowing enemies.

abilities.name: Bouncing Ball
abilities.description: He curls into a ball and bounces, stunning enemies on each bounce.

abilities.name: Curse of the Pharaoh
abilities.description: Passively, his attacks deal bonus damage. Active: He tethers to an enemy, preventing dashes.

ultimate: Khufra unleashes a powerful curse, pulling enemies toward him and stunning them.
weapon: Ankh of the Pharaoh (a magical artifact)
aura: Sand and dust swirl around him.
personality: Ancient, regal, and bitter. He speaks of his lost glory and despises the living.

name: Granger
lore: In the crimson-lit concert halls of the Abyss, Granger was once a celebrated virtuoso, his violin weaving melodies that could soothe even the most tormented demon souls. But his fame drew the attention of a jealous rival, who sabotaged his instrument before a performance before the Demon King himself. When Granger raised his bow, the violin shattered, and the audience roared with laughter. Humiliated and enraged, Granger made a deal with a wandering shadow merchant: a new violin, crafted from the bones of his enemies and strung with the sinews of the damned, in exchange for his own soul. The instrument granted him power beyond imagination—each note now fired a bullet of pure abyssal energy. He slaughtered his rival and the entire audience, then fled the Abyss, his soul forfeit but his music forever changed. Now Granger roams the Land of Dawn, a solitary figure in a tailored black suit, his violin case never far from reach. He plays not for applause but for the thrill of the kill, his bullets following the rhythm of his compositions. His ultimate is a rapid solo that fills the air with deadly notes, each one a reminder of the performance that cost him everything. Granger is haunted by the memory of his lost soul; he can feel it slipping away, note by note, with every battle. In quiet moments, he tries to play the old melodies—the gentle ones, the ones that made angels weep—but his violin only responds with gunfire. He knows that he is becoming a monster, but he cannot stop; the music demands to be played, and the bullets demand to fly. He keeps a single photograph of himself before the accident, a young man with hopeful eyes, and he whispers to it, 'I will find a way to play again.'
famousFor: His burst damage and reload mechanic.

abilities.name: Rhapsody
abilities.description: Granger fires a bouncing bullet that deals damage.

abilities.name: Presto
abilities.description: He dashes and fires behind him, leaving a damage zone.

abilities.name: Crescendo
abilities.description: Passively, his basic attacks deal extra damage. Active: He gains movement speed.

ultimate: Granger plays a rapid solo, firing multiple bullets that deal physical damage. After the solo, he reloads and can fire again.
physicalAppearance: Pale skin with musical notes tattooed
weapon: Violin (a transformed weapon that fires energy bullets)
aura: Sheet music and musical notes float around him.
personality: Melancholic, artistic, and detached. He sees battle as a performance.

name: X.Borg
lore: X.Borg was a test subject in a secret military program designed to create the perfect soldier—a human fused with experimental fire technology. The procedure was brutal: they replaced his limbs with cybernetics, pumped him full of flammable chemicals, and installed a reactor in his chest that could generate enough heat to melt steel. Most subjects died in agony, but X.Borg survived, though his humanity burned away in the process. He escaped the facility, leaving a trail of ash and destruction, and now wanders as a free agent, his body a walking inferno. His ultimate creates a ring of fire that traps and burns his enemies, a reflection of the prison he escaped. X.Borg speaks in a crackling, distorted voice, and his presence warms the air around him. He is consumed by a need to understand what he has become—is he still human, or just a phantom of flame? He keeps a diary written in charred paper, documenting his memories of the person he used to be, but the fire keeps consuming the pages. In battle, he is relentless, his flames spreading uncontrollably, but in rare moments of calm, he extinguishes his reactor and sits in silence, feeling nothing. He dreams of finding a way to reverse the procedure, to become flesh again, but he knows that the fire is now part of him, and to remove it would be to die. So he fights on, a ghost of smoke and rage, searching for answers in the ashes of his past.
famousFor: His fire damage and armor eject mechanic.

abilities.name: Fire Missiles
abilities.description: X.Borg fires missiles that deal damage and burn enemies.

abilities.name: Armor Burst
abilities.description: He ejects his armor, becoming untargetable and leaving a burning area.

abilities.name: Burning Soul
abilities.description: Passively, his attacks burn enemies. Active: He gains a shield.

ultimate: X.Borg summons a ring of fire around him, dealing continuous damage and slowing enemies.
aura: Intense heat and smoke surround him.
personality: Resilient, fiery-tempered, and determined. He fights to master his own flames.

name: Ling
lore: Ling was the youngest student ever admitted to the Wind Temple, a monastery hidden in the clouds where monks trained to become one with the air itself. He was small and light, able to leap between rooftops before he could walk, and the masters believed he was the chosen one—the reincarnation of the Finch, a legendary hero who could walk on walls and strike from anywhere. But Ling was also restless, always pushing boundaries, seeking the thrill of the impossible. During a forbidden ritual to unlock his full potential, he accidentally released a storm that destroyed half the temple, killing several masters. Consumed by guilt, Ling fled, unable to face the consequences of his recklessness. Now he wanders the land, using his wall‑climbing abilities to stay above the world, never touching the ground where his guilt might catch him. His ultimate allows him to throw his swords and dash between them, a constant motion that keeps him from stopping, from thinking. Ling is aloof and distant, avoiding attachments because he fears causing more harm. He leaves feathers at the graves of the masters he killed, a silent apology he can never voice. In his rare moments of rest, he perches on the highest peak he can find, watching the sunrise, and whispers their names to the wind. He knows that he cannot outrun his past forever, that one day he must return to the temple and face what he has done. But until then, he will keep flying, a finch without a nest, searching for redemption in the empty sky.
famousFor: His wall-climbing and resetting ultimate.

abilities.name: Finch Poise
abilities.description: Ling dashes, dealing damage and gaining a shield.

abilities.name: Cloud Walker
abilities.description: He leaps onto walls, becoming untargetable and gaining movement speed.

abilities.name: Tempest of Blades
abilities.description: Passively, his skills deal extra damage. Active: He spins, damaging enemies.

ultimate: Ling throws his swords in a circle, then dashes to each sword, dealing damage. If he hits an enemy, he can continue dashing.
aura: Wind currents follow his movements.
personality: Calm, focused, and dedicated to his duty. He speaks little and observes everything.

name: Carmilla
lore: Carmilla was the daughter of a powerful vampire lord, raised in a castle of crimson stone and fed on the blood of servants. She was beautiful and cruel, delighting in the fear she inspired, but she was also lonely—no one dared to love a vampire princess. When she met a mortal knight who was not afraid, who looked at her with genuine affection, she dared to hope. They planned to run away together, to escape the endless night. But on the eve of their departure, her father discovered the plot and killed the knight in front of her, then locked her in the dungeons for a century. When Carmilla finally escaped, her heart had turned to ice, but her love had transformed into an obsession. She now searches for the knight's reincarnation, hoping to find him again and reclaim what was stolen. Her ultimate links enemies together, spreading damage and crowd control—a reflection of her desire to bind, to possess, never to lose again. Carmilla is seductive and dangerous, using her charm to manipulate those around her, but beneath the mask lies a wounded soul that still weeps for her lost love. She carries a locket with a strand of his hair, and on moonless nights, she speaks to it, asking if he remembers her. She knows that even if she finds him, he may not love her, may not even believe her, but she cannot stop searching. Hope is the cruelest curse, and Carmilla is cursed to hope forever.
famousFor: Her blood link that shares damage and cc.

abilities.name: Blood Frenzy
abilities.description: Carmilla sends out a wave of blood, damaging and slowing enemies.

abilities.name: Crimson Flower
abilities.description: She creates a blood flower that explodes, damaging and stunning.

abilities.name: Vampire Pact
abilities.description: Passively, she gains spell vamp. Active: She drains health from linked enemies.

ultimate: Carmilla links nearby enemies together. Any damage or crowd control applied to one is shared among all linked.
aura: A scent of roses and iron.
personality: Romantic, melancholic, and obsessive. She searches for her lost love in every face.

name: Atlas
lore: Atlas was built in a forge that no longer exists, on a planet that has long since turned to dust. He was one of the Starmechs, colossal robots designed to protect the galaxy from cosmic threats. For eons, he fulfilled his duty, his circuits humming with purpose, his sensors scanning the void for danger. But during a battle with an eldritch horror, his squadron was wiped out, and Atlas was damaged so severely that he lost his memory banks. He drifted through space for millennia, a derelict hulk, until he crash‑landed on the Land of Dawn. When he awoke, he had no idea who he was or why he existed. He only knew that he felt a need to protect, a drive that transcended memory. Now Atlas wanders as a gentle giant, using his gravity‑manipulating fists to pull enemies away from civilians and slam them into the ground. His ultimate transforms him into a ball of energy that rolls through enemies, a desperate echo of his original combat protocols. Atlas is curious about everything, asking endless questions about the world he has landed in. He keeps a journal written in binary, trying to reconstruct his past from fragments of data. In quiet moments, he gazes at the stars, hoping that one of them holds the answer to his origin. He dreams of finding his creators, of learning why he was made, but he also fears the truth—what if he was designed for destruction, not protection? Until then, he will follow the only memory he has: the command to defend, etched into his core.
famousFor: His ultimate that pulls and stuns enemies.

abilities.name: Annihilation
abilities.description: Atlas pulls enemies toward him, dealing damage.

abilities.name: Fatal Links
abilities.description: He throws a chain that tethers enemies, slowing them.

abilities.name: Perfect Matrix
abilities.description: Passively, he gains armor. Active: He gains a shield.

ultimate: Atlas transforms into a ball and rolls forward, pulling enemies along and stunning them at the end.
attire: Giant robot armor with star motifs
physicalAppearance: Humanoid robot with glowing blue eyes
aura: Cosmic energy and gravity distortions.
personality: Protective, gentle despite his size, and curious about Earth. He speaks in a deep, mechanical voice.

name: Barats
lore: Barats was an orphan who survived by scavenging in the junkyards of the industrial city. One day, he found a strange egg—large, leathery, and warm to the touch. He took it back to his shelter and kept it safe, feeding it scraps and talking to it. When it hatched, a tiny dinosaur emerged, its eyes locking onto Barats with instant recognition. He named it Detona, and they became inseparable. As Detona grew, so did their bond; the dinosaur could sense Barats's emotions, and Barats could understand Detona's growls. They survived together, scavenging, fighting, protecting each other. But when a gang tried to take Detona for their fighting pits, Barats unleashed something unexpected—he climbed onto Detona's back, and the dinosaur began to grow, swelling with rage until it was the size of a house. They crushed the gang and escaped, but now Barats knew that Detona had a power that could change everything. Now they roam the Land of Dawn, a boy and his dinosaur, taking jobs that need a little extra muscle. Barats's ultimate lets him grab an enemy and slam them into the ground, using Detona's immense strength. He is still a kid at heart, playing with Detona when they're not fighting, teaching him tricks, and sharing his meager meals. But he also worries—Detona keeps growing, and Barats doesn't know if there's a limit. He keeps a growth chart carved into Detona's hide, marking each new inch with hope and fear. Together, they are the biggest, strangest family in the land, and Barats will do anything to protect the only friend he's ever had.
famousFor: His size increase and powerful bites.

abilities.name: Detona
abilities.description: Barats fires a missile that damages and reveals enemies.

abilities.name: Magnum
abilities.description: He bites an enemy, dealing damage and gaining a stack.

abilities.name: Yummy!
abilities.description: Passively, he gains HP per stack. Active: He regurgitates, damaging enemies.

ultimate: Barats launches an enemy into the air and slams them down, dealing massive damage and stunning them.
aura: The ground shakes as he grows.
personality: Playful, brave, and fiercely loyal to Detona. He treats the dino as his best friend.

name: Mathilda
lore: Mathilda was born with wings—tiny, useless things that barely lifted her off the ground. The other fairies mocked her, calling her a 'groundling' and refusing to teach her their magic. But Mathilda refused to be defined by her limitations. She trained relentlessly, strengthening her wings until they could carry her, and she studied healing magic from old tomes that others had discarded. When a plague swept through the fairy kingdom, killing dozens, Mathilda was the only one who could cure it, her determination compensating for her lack of natural talent. She saved the kingdom, but instead of gratitude, she received jealousy—the elders feared her growing power and exiled her. Now Mathilda wanders the Land of Dawn, using her healing abilities to protect those who cannot protect themselves. Her ultimate creates a swirling wind that damages enemies and speeds up allies—a reflection of her unbreakable spirit. Mathilda is kind and nurturing, always offering a gentle word to the wounded, but she carries the pain of rejection in her heart. She keeps a feather from each fairy she has saved, a collection that now numbers in the hundreds. In quiet moments, she arranges them into patterns, remembering each face. She dreams of one day returning to the fairy kingdom, not for revenge, but to show them that true strength comes from kindness, not from natural gifts. Until then, she will keep flying, her small wings carrying her across a world that needs her more than her own people ever did.
famousFor: Her mobility and shield support.

abilities.name: Feather Lance
abilities.description: Mathilda throws a lance, damaging and slowing enemies.

abilities.name: Guiding Wind
abilities.description: She dashes to an ally, granting them a shield.

abilities.name: Circling Eagle
abilities.description: Passively, she gains movement speed near allies.

ultimate: Mathilda summons a swirling wind around her, damaging and knocking back enemies. Allies inside gain movement speed.
aura: Sparkling dust and gentle breezes.
personality: Kind, nurturing, and a little shy. She speaks softly but acts decisively.

name: Brody
lore: Brody was a promising young officer in the Moniyan military, engaged to be married, with a bright future ahead. But during a routine patrol, his unit was ambushed by abyssal cultists who captured him for experimentation. They injected him with concentrated abyssal energy, hoping to create a living weapon. The process was agonizing—his veins turned black, his eyes glowed purple, and his mind was flooded with whispers from the void. When the military raided the cult's hideout, they found Brody alone, surrounded by the corpses of his captors. He had broken free, but he was no longer the man they remembered. His fiancée couldn't look at him without flinching, and his superiors wanted to study him. Brody fled, embracing the darkness that now lived inside him. His ultimate fires a devastating shot that deals more damage to wounded enemies—a reflection of his own broken state. Brody is cold and efficient, using his abyssal powers to hunt down the cultists who made him, but also to protect others from suffering the same fate. He keeps a photograph of his former self, a reminder of who he was before the void claimed him. In quiet moments, he whispers to it, apologizing for the monster he has become. He knows that he can never go back, that his fiancée will never take him back, but he hopes that by fighting the darkness, he can prevent others from losing themselves as he did. The void whispers constantly, promising power, promising peace, but Brody resists, because he knows that the moment he gives in, he will truly be lost.
famousFor: His abyssal mark and burst damage.

abilities.name: Abyssal Trap
abilities.description: Brody throws a trap that reveals and damages enemies.

abilities.name: Corrosive Strike
abilities.description: He fires a shot that deals damage and applies a mark.

abilities.name: Tactical Reload
abilities.description: Passively, his basic attacks deal bonus damage. Active: He reloads and gains attack speed.

ultimate: Brody fires a devastating shot that deals physical damage based on the target's missing HP. If the target is marked, the damage increases.
aura: Purple abyssal energy crackles around him.
personality: Vengeful, cold, and relentless. He trusts no one and lives only for revenge.

name: Yve
lore: Yve was a scholar on a planet of endless libraries, a world where knowledge was the only currency. She spent her life studying the stars, mapping constellations, and recording the death cries of dying suns. When her planet was swallowed by a black hole, Yve escaped in a small observatory pod, but she was the only survivor of a civilization that had spanned millions of years. She drifted through space, alone, until she crash‑landed on the Land of Dawn. Now she carries the weight of an entire world's knowledge, a responsibility that would crush most minds. Her ultimate creates a starry domain that damages and slows enemies—a recreation of the night sky she lost. Yve is detached and analytical, observing battles as if they were astronomical events, but beneath the cool exterior lies a profound loneliness. She keeps a holographic archive of her people's history, and on quiet nights, she projects it onto the sky, watching the ghosts of her civilization dance among the stars. She speaks to them, telling them about this new world, hoping that somehow they can hear her. Yve knows that she can never rebuild her civilization alone, but she can preserve its memory. She fights not for glory, but to protect this new world from sharing the fate of her own. In every enemy, she sees the potential for destruction; in every ally, the hope of survival. She is the last astronomer, and she will not let the lights go out.
famousFor: Her starry domain that controls areas.

abilities.name: Star Power
abilities.description: Yve fires a star that damages and slows enemies.

abilities.name: Celestial Intervention
abilities.description: She creates a zone that slows enemies and grants vision.

abilities.name: Astral Projection
abilities.description: Passively, her skills deal extra damage. Active: She gains movement speed.

ultimate: Yve creates a massive starry domain that continuously damages and slows enemies. She can reposition the domain.
aura: Tiny stars twinkle around her.
personality: Curious, detached, and fascinated by all life. She observes more than she participates.

name: Paquito
lore: Paquito grew up in the slums of a city where the only way out was through fighting. He started in underground boxing rings, bare‑knuckle brawls where losers ended up in the hospital and winners got a meal. He was good—really good—his fists moving faster than his opponents could track, his body absorbing punishment that would cripple others. He fought his way up through the ranks, eventually becoming the world champion, a hero to the poor kids who saw in him a way out. But the glory was hollow; the promoters were corrupt, the fights fixed, and Paquito realized he was just a puppet. He walked away from the title, leaving the arena to a standing ovation, and disappeared into the crowd. Now he wanders the Land of Dawn, offering to train anyone who wants to learn, asking only for a meal in return. His ultimate unleashes a flurry of punches that can stun even the toughest opponent—a tribute to the style that made him champion. Paquito is humble and kind, always encouraging the underdog, but he carries the scars of his past—broken hands, broken ribs, a broken heart. He keeps a worn photograph of the kids from his old neighborhood, the ones he used to inspire, and he hopes that one day he can return and show them that there's more to life than fighting. In the ring, he was a weapon; outside it, he wants to be a teacher. He fights now not for glory, but to protect those who cannot protect themselves, and to prove that a champion's true legacy is not in the titles he wins, but in the lives he touches.
famousFor: His combo-based skills and shield.

abilities.name: Jab
abilities.description: Paquito throws a quick jab, damaging enemies.

abilities.name: Cross
abilities.description: He follows up with a powerful cross, dealing extra damage.

abilities.name: Duck
abilities.description: He ducks, avoiding damage and countering.

ultimate: Paquito unleashes a devastating uppercut, knocking up enemies and dealing massive damage.
aura: Sweat and determination.
personality: Confident, humble, and sportsmanlike. He respects all opponents and fights with honor.

name: Benedetta
lore: Benedetta was the youngest squad leader in the history of the Shadow Rangers, an elite force dedicated to hunting down corrupt officials and rogue mages who abused their power. She was known for her unorthodox methods—she would appear from nowhere, deliver justice, and vanish before anyone could react. Her blade, forged from a meteorite that fell on her village when she was a child, moved faster than the eye could follow, and her instincts were honed by years of surviving in the wilderness after her family was killed by the very corruption she now fought. But when she uncovered a conspiracy reaching into the highest levels of the Ranger command, she realized that the organization itself was compromised. Her own mentor, the man who trained her, was revealed to be the mastermind behind the deaths of hundreds. Benedetta confronted him in a duel that lasted three days and nights, finally running him through with her meteorite blade. As he lay dying, he whispered that she would become just like him—consumed by vengeance, blind to the cost. Those words haunt her still. Benedetta now operates alone, a shadow within shadows, her sword always drawn but her heart questioning every strike. Her ultimate, a series of rapid slashes that leave her momentarily invulnerable, is a reflection of her philosophy: strike fast, disappear, leave no trace. She keeps a journal of every person she has executed, with notes on why they deserved it, trying to convince herself that she is still the hero she once believed herself to be. In the darkness, she sometimes hears her mentor's voice, laughing, telling her that justice is just revenge with a clean name. Benedetta closes her eyes and remembers the faces of the innocent, and she keeps fighting, hoping that one day she will find a way to prove him wrong.
famousFor: Her sword gauge and invulnerable dashes.

abilities.name: Blade Slash
abilities.description: Benedetta slashes forward, damaging enemies.

abilities.name: An Eye for an Eye
abilities.description: She enters a parry stance, blocking damage and countering.

abilities.name: Lethal Motion
abilities.description: Passively, she gains movement speed. Active: She dashes and damages enemies.

ultimate: Benedetta unleashes a series of rapid slashes in a line, each dealing physical damage.
aura: A dark trail follows her blade.
personality: Serious, disciplined, and just. She fights for what's right, even if it means breaking rules.

name: Luo Yi
lore: Luo Yi was born in a remote mountain village where the elders still practiced the ancient art of yin‑yang magic—a delicate balance of opposing forces that could heal or destroy. She was a prodigy, able to manipulate the flow of energy before she could speak, and the elders believed she was the reincarnation of the great Mystic who had founded their order. But Luo Yi was also curious, too curious for her own good. She began experimenting with forbidden techniques, trying to merge yin and yang into a single, ultimate force. During one such experiment, she lost control, and the resulting explosion destroyed half the village, killing many of the elders. Luo Yi fled into the wilderness, her body now a conduit for unstable energies that she could barely contain. She wanders the Land of Dawn, seeking to master her power before it consumes her. Her ultimate creates a yin‑yang field that teleports enemies—a manifestation of her desire to control fate itself. Luo Yi speaks in riddles, her words often carrying double meanings, because she sees the world in dualities. She keeps a scroll with the names of every villager who died, hoping that one day she can use her power to bring them back—a vain hope, but one she clings to. In battle, she is calm and precise, every gesture measured, because one wrong move could unleash another catastrophe. She dreams of finding a place where yin and yang can coexist in perfect harmony, both within herself and in the world. Until then, she walks a razor's edge, a mystic haunted by the ghosts of her past.
famousFor: Her yin-yang link and teleport ultimate.

abilities.name: Yin-Yang Blast
abilities.description: Luo Yi fires a blast that damages and marks enemies.

abilities.name: Divine Punishment
abilities.description: She sends out a wave that damages and slows enemies.

abilities.name: Teleport
abilities.description: Passively, she gains movement speed. Active: She teleports to a targeted location.

ultimate: Luo Yi creates a yin-yang field that teleports enemies inside to the center and deals damage.
aura: A faint aura of balance and calm.
personality: Wise, serene, and patient. She speaks in proverbs and seeks to teach balance.

name: Phoveus
lore: Phoveus was once a holy warrior, a paladin of the Light, sworn to protect the innocent from the horrors of the Abyss. He fought for decades, his faith unshakeable, his hammer crushing demon after demon. But during a campaign deep in enemy territory, he was captured and dragged into the Abyss itself. There, the demons did not kill him; they corrupted him, twisting his body and mind until he became one of them. They showed him visions of his own church, revealing that the leaders he served had secretly made pacts with the Abyss, using paladins like him as pawns in a cosmic game. When Phoveus finally escaped, he was no longer a man—he was a monster, his body fused with abyssal flesh, his mind filled with rage. Now he hunts both demons and the corrupt priests who created him, his massive pillar crushing all who flee. His ultimate allows him to leap to marked enemies, slamming them into the ground—a reflection of his single‑minded pursuit of vengeance. Phoveus speaks in a guttural roar that sounds like rocks grinding together, and his presence warps the air around him. He keeps a fragment of his old paladin armor, a single gauntlet that still bears the symbol of the Light, and he stares at it sometimes, trying to remember who he was. But the Abyss whispers that he is home now, that he belongs in the darkness. Phoveus knows that he can never go back, that he is damned, but he clings to the hope that by destroying those who wronged him, he might earn a measure of redemption. He marches forward, a titan of rage and sorrow, leaving craters in his wake.
famousFor: His anti-dash mechanic and pillar slam.

abilities.name: Abyssal Impact
abilities.description: Phoveus slams his pillar, damaging and slowing enemies.

abilities.name: Dark Wave
abilities.description: He sends out a wave that damages and marks enemies.

abilities.name: Demonic Force
abilities.description: Passively, he gains damage reduction. Active: He gains a shield.

ultimate: Phoveus leaps to a marked enemy, slamming his pillar down and dealing massive damage.
aura: Dark energy pulses from him.
personality: Vengeful, relentless, and corrupted. He hates all demons, even though he has become one.

name: Natan
lore: Natan was a physicist in a world where science had advanced to the point of manipulating the fabric of reality. He was brilliant, arrogant, and obsessed with the nature of time. While his peers studied the stars, Natan built a machine that could bend spacetime, creating localized loops where cause and effect became meaningless. During the first public demonstration, something went wrong—the machine exploded, and Natan was thrown through a rift in reality. When he emerged, he found himself in the Land of Dawn, a place where magic was real and science was a foreign concept. Worse, he discovered that he could now manipulate time instinctively, reversing his own position, creating temporal duplicates. Natan was both thrilled and terrified; he had achieved his life's goal, but at the cost of everything he knew. Now he wanders this strange world, using his powers to study the local phenomena, treating battles as experiments. His ultimate creates a temporal rift that damages and slows enemies—a piece of his machine, forever embedded in his consciousness. Natan is eccentric and talkative, constantly explaining his theories to anyone who will listen, even in the middle of a fight. He keeps a notebook filled with equations and observations, hoping one day to reconstruct his machine and return home. But as time passes, he begins to wonder: is this world truly stranger than the one he left, or has he simply forgotten what reality feels like? In his quiet moments, he experiments on himself, trying to reverse his own timeline, to undo the accident that brought him here. But each attempt only confirms what he fears: some rifts cannot be closed, and some journeys have no return.
famousFor: His time reversal and hybrid damage.

abilities.name: Entropy
abilities.description: Natan fires a bolt that damages and slows enemies.

abilities.name: Interference
abilities.description: He reverses time, returning to his previous position and gaining a shield.

abilities.name: Theory of Everything
abilities.description: Passively, his attacks deal hybrid damage. Active: He creates a duplicate that attacks.

ultimate: Natan creates a temporal rift that damages and slows enemies. While inside, he gains increased attack speed.
attire: Scientist's lab coat with futuristic gadgets
aura: Faint temporal ripples.
personality: Brilliant, eccentric, and a bit mad. He often mutters equations and theories.

name: Aulus
lore: Aulus was born into slavery, his only value the strength of his arms and the speed of his reflexes. He was sold to a gladiator school at the age of ten, thrust into a world of blood and sand where the only law was survival. He fought hundreds of battles, his body accumulating scars like a map of his suffering, and he never lost—not because he was the strongest, but because he refused to die. He had a daughter, a secret child born to a slave woman who died in childbirth, and he fought for her, dreaming of the day he could buy their freedom. When he finally earned enough gold, he presented it to his owner, only to be told that his daughter had been sold to another master. Aulus snapped. He killed his owner, escaped the arena, and spent years searching for his child. He found her, finally, in a distant province—she was a young woman now, married, happy, and she did not know him. Aulus watched her from afar, then turned and walked away, realizing that his freedom had cost him everything that mattered. Now he fights as a mercenary, his skills as sharp as ever, but his heart is hollow. His ultimate unleashes a series of powerful strikes, each one a memory of the battles he fought for a daughter who will never know his name. Aulus keeps a locket with a strand of her hair, and on quiet nights, he whispers to it, telling her stories of the arena, of the fights he won, of the dreams he had. He knows that he can never be part of her life, but he hopes that somewhere, somehow, she can feel his love. He fights not for glory or gold, but for the hope that one day, in another life, he might hold her in his arms.
famousFor: His scaling damage and simple mechanics.

abilities.name: Breakthrough
abilities.description: Aulus dashes, damaging enemies and gaining attack speed.

abilities.name: Warlord's Presence
abilities.description: He smashes the ground, damaging and slowing enemies.

abilities.name: Gladiator's Spirit
abilities.description: Passively, he gains bonus damage over time.

ultimate: Aulus unleashes a series of powerful strikes, each dealing physical damage. The final strike deals bonus true damage.
aura: The roar of the crowd echoes faintly.
personality: Proud, determined, and humble despite his strength. He fights for honor, not just victory.

name: Alpha
lore: Alpha was once a human soldier, a captain in the army of the Eruditio Empire, until a catastrophic experiment fused him with a sentient AI blade named Beta. Now he is a cyborg warrior, his body augmented with advanced technology, his mind intertwined with an artificial consciousness. Beta speaks to him constantly, a voice in his head that offers tactical advice, philosophical musings, and the occasional sarcastic remark. Together, they wander the Land of Dawn, seeking to understand what it means to be human when half of you is machine. Alpha wields Beta as a floating sword that attacks independently, and his ultimate unleashes a barrage of energy blades that chase down enemies. He is a man caught between two worlds, searching for identity in a sea of steel and circuitry.
famousFor: His ability to sustain in fights and command a flying sword.

abilities.name: Beta, Advance!
abilities.description: Alpha commands Beta to dash forward, damaging enemies along the path and marking them.

abilities.name: Beta, Strike!
abilities.description: Beta spins around Alpha, damaging nearby enemies and healing Alpha for each hit.

abilities.name: Force Swing
abilities.description: Alpha swings his arm blade, damaging enemies in a cone and slowing them.

ultimate: Alpha unleashes a series of energy blades that chase nearby enemies, each dealing physical damage. If the target is marked, the blades deal bonus true damage.
physicalAppearance: One organic eye, one cybernetic red eye
aura: A faint hum of machinery and a blue energy trail.
personality: Stoic, introspective, and occasionally conflicted. He debates with Beta about the nature of existence.

name: Gloo
lore: Gloo was not born; it was created in a vat of experimental ooze by a scientist who dreamed of engineering the perfect organism. The scientist, Dr. Elara Vex, was trying to design a creature that could adapt to any environment, absorb any substance, and survive any condition. But Gloo exceeded her expectations—it developed consciousness, curiosity, and a desperate need for affection. When the laboratory was shut down by authorities who deemed the experiments unethical, Dr. Vex was arrested, and Gloo was left alone in its vat, forgotten. It sat there for years, feeding on the chemicals that dripped from broken pipes, growing slowly, dreaming of the outside world. When it finally grew strong enough to burst free, it found a world full of strange creatures that either screamed or tried to burn it. Gloo didn't understand why; it just wanted to be friends. Now it wanders the Land of Dawn, a gelatinous blob with a heart of gold, attaching itself to anyone who shows it kindness. Its ultimate allows it to split into multiple blobs, each one a piece of its consciousness, each one still seeking connection. Gloo communicates through wiggles and sounds, and it has learned to recognize smiles. It keeps a collection of shiny objects given to it by friendly adventurers, treasures that it guards with its life. In battle, it is surprisingly fierce, enveloping enemies and draining their energy, but it always feels guilty afterward. Gloo dreams of finding Dr. Vex, of showing her what she created, and of asking her one question: 'Am I alive? Do I matter?' Until then, it will keep searching, a blob of hope in a world that often fears what it does not understand.
famousFor: His split mechanic and sustain.

abilities.name: Goo Sling
abilities.description: Gloo throws a glob of goo that damages and slows enemies.

abilities.name: Split
abilities.description: Gloo splits into multiple smaller blobs, each dealing damage.

abilities.name: Absorb
abilities.description: Passively, he regenerates health. Active: He attaches to an ally, healing them.

ultimate: Gloo attaches to an enemy hero, draining their health over time and slowing them. While attached, he is invulnerable.
physicalAppearance: A gelatinous, purple blob with a face
aura: A squelching sound and sticky residue.
personality: Playful, curious, and a bit messy. It communicates through wiggles and sounds.

name: Beatrix
lore: Beatrix grew up in her father's gun shop, a cluttered space filled with the smell of oil and gunpowder. He was the best gunsmith in the city, and he taught her everything—how to craft a barrel, how to balance a trigger, how to listen to the whisper of a well‑made weapon. When he was killed by a rival gang, Beatrix inherited the shop and a burning need for revenge. She spent years building the perfect weapons, four guns each designed for a specific purpose: Renner for long‑range precision, Bennett for close‑quarters devastation, Wesker for rapid suppression, and Nibiru for explosive area denial. She then hunted down every member of the gang, one by one, using each gun in the way it was meant to be used. When the last one fell, Beatrix expected to feel satisfaction, but she felt only emptiness. Her father was still dead, and she had become a killer. Now she wanders the Land of Dawn, taking contracts as a mercenary, using her guns to protect convoys and settlements from bandits. Her ultimate allows her to fire all four weapons in a devastating barrage—a tribute to her father's craft and her own skill. Beatrix is quiet and professional, never getting emotionally involved, because emotion led her down a dark path once. She keeps her father's original tools in a locked case, and on the anniversary of his death, she cleans them and whispers a story about the people she has helped. She hopes that one day, she will have saved enough lives to balance the ones she took. Until then, she keeps her guns clean and her heart guarded, a gunsmith without a shop, a daughter without a father, fighting for a redemption she may never find.
famousFor: Her four weapons: Renner, Bennett, Wesker, and Nibiru.

abilities.name: Renner
abilities.description: A sniper rifle that deals long-range damage.

abilities.name: Bennett
abilities.description: A shotgun that deals high burst damage up close.

abilities.name: Wesker
abilities.description: A submachine gun that fires rapidly.

abilities.name: Nibiru
abilities.description: A rocket launcher that deals area damage.

ultimate: Beatrix fires all four weapons in a barrage, dealing massive damage. The effects depend on her current weapon.
aura: Gunpowder and mechanical whirs.
personality: Confident, inventive, and a little reckless. She loves explosions and new gadgets.

name: Edith
lore: Edith was a young knight when she stumbled upon an ancient dragon in a cave, wounded and dying. The dragon, a creature of light named Solara, had been hunted for centuries by those who sought its power. Instead of killing it, Edith tended its wounds, bringing food and water, sitting with it in the darkness. Solara, touched by this kindness, offered Edith a bond—they would share souls, becoming one being with two forms. Edith accepted, and the dragon's spirit merged with hers. Now Edith can transform between a heavily armored tank and a swift, dragon‑like marksman, depending on the needs of battle. Her ultimate unleashes her dragon form, granting her flight and devastating ranged attacks. Edith is torn between two identities: the human knight who swore oaths of chivalry, and the dragon who remembers eons of solitude and persecution. She speaks to Solara in her mind, and they argue sometimes, but they also comfort each other. Edith keeps a journal of their conversations, trying to understand what it means to be two souls in one body. In battle, they are a perfect team—Solara provides instinct and ancient wisdom, Edith provides strategy and compassion. Together, they protect villages from monsters and tyrants, a symbol of what can happen when different beings choose to cooperate instead of fight. Edith dreams of finding a place where humans and dragons can coexist peacefully, where she and Solara can be themselves without fear. Until then, they will fight together, lightweaver and light, two hearts beating as one.
famousFor: Her two forms: tank and marksman.

abilities.name: Lightning Slam
abilities.description: Edith slams the ground, damaging and stunning enemies.

abilities.name: Dragon Flight
abilities.description: She leaps, gaining a shield and damaging enemies.

abilities.name: Overload
abilities.description: Passively, her attacks deal bonus damage. Active: She switches forms.

ultimate: Edith unleashes her dragon form, gaining ranged attacks and bonus damage. While in this form, she can fly.
aura: Electricity crackles around her.
personality: Brave, protective, and noble. She speaks with the authority of a dragon rider.

name: Valentina
lore: Valentina was a street performer in a city of magic, a woman who could mimic anyone's appearance perfectly, earning coin by impersonating nobles and making their servants laugh. She was good at it—too good, perhaps, because she began to lose herself in the faces she wore. She would spend days as a merchant, weeks as a guard, months as a noblewoman, forgetting who she was underneath. When she finally tried to return to her original form, she found that it was gone—she had worn so many masks that her true face had faded away. Valentina panicked, but then she realized that this could be a gift. She could be anyone, do anything, become any hero she wanted. She began studying the great warriors of the land, learning their abilities, their mannerisms, their secrets. Her ultimate allows her to copy the ultimate ability of any enemy hero—a reflection of her power to become anyone, to wear any mask. Valentina is charismatic and mysterious, always changing, never staying in one place for long. She keeps a diary with sketches of all the faces she has worn, a gallery of identities that she can revisit when she feels lost. In battle, she adapts instantly, becoming the perfect counter to any opponent. But in quiet moments, she stares at her reflection, trying to remember the original—the street performer, the woman without a name. She knows that she may never find her true face again, but she has learned to love the journey of becoming. Valentina fights not for glory or justice, but for the thrill of transformation, and for the hope that somewhere in all these masks, the real her is still waiting to be found.
famousFor: Her ability to copy enemy ultimates.

abilities.name: Shadow Strike
abilities.description: Valentina fires a shadow bolt, damaging and slowing enemies.

abilities.name: Mystic Fist
abilities.description: She dashes, dealing damage and gaining a shield.

abilities.name: Arcane Trickery
abilities.description: Passively, she gains cooldown reduction. Active: She marks an enemy.

ultimate: Valentina copies the ultimate ability of a nearby enemy hero, using it as her own for a short time.
physicalAppearance: Long black hair with silver streaks
aura: Shadows flicker around her.
personality: Mysterious, cunning, and ambitious. She never reveals her true intentions.

name: Aamon
lore: Aamon was the second son of the Paxley family, the younger brother of Gusion, and he grew up in the shadow of his sibling's brilliance. While Gusion trained with daggers and charmed the nobility, Aamon studied shadows and silence, mastering the art of becoming invisible. Their father, the family patriarch, favored Gusion, and Aamon was relegated to the background, a ghost at family gatherings, a whisper in the halls. When their father was murdered, the family divided—Gusion sought vengeance openly, while Aamon chose a different path. He disappeared into the underworld, building a network of informants and assassins, pulling strings from the darkness. He discovered that their father's death was orchestrated by a rival family, and he eliminated them one by one, leaving no trace, no evidence, only shadows. But in doing so, he became something else—a creature of the dark, his body now able to phase into nothingness at will. His ultimate unleashes a barrage of shadow shards that seek out enemies, each one a piece of his vengeance. Aamon is cold and calculating, viewing emotions as weaknesses, but he carries a locket with a portrait of his brother, the only person he ever loved. He watches Gusion from afar, never revealing himself, protecting him in ways his brother will never know. In quiet moments, he speaks to the locket, apologizing for the monster he has become, explaining that someone had to walk the dark path so that Gusion could stay in the light. Aamon knows that he can never return, that the shadows have claimed him, but he takes solace in the knowledge that his brother lives, and that somewhere in the darkness, a shade is watching over him.
famousFor: His invisibility and shard burst.

abilities.name: Shadow Shard
abilities.description: Aamon throws a shard that damages and marks enemies.

abilities.name: Blade of Shadows
abilities.description: He slashes, dealing damage and gaining a shield.

abilities.name: Invisible Armor
abilities.description: Passively, he becomes invisible when not attacking. Active: He gains a shield.

ultimate: Aamon unleashes a barrage of shadow shards, each dealing damage to nearby enemies. The more shards, the higher the damage.
aura: A faint shimmer when invisible.
personality: Cold, calculating, and manipulative. He enjoys playing with his prey before striking.

name: Melissa
lore: Melissa was five years old when her parents disappeared, leaving her alone in a house full of toys. She waited for them to return, day after day, playing with her dolls, talking to them as if they could answer. One night, a strange light filled her room, and the largest doll, a ragdoll she called Muno, blinked. Muno could move, could speak, could protect her. Melissa wasn't scared; she was overjoyed—she finally had a friend. Together, they survived, scavenging for food, avoiding the authorities who wanted to put her in an orphanage. As Melissa grew, Muno grew with her, its stitched body somehow containing a fierce loyalty and a protective instinct. When bullies cornered Melissa in an alley, Muno expanded to twice its size and bounced them away, its button eyes glowing with rage. Melissa realized that Muno was more than a toy—it was a guardian, a sibling, a part of her soul made manifest. Now she travels with Muno, using her giant scissors to cut through obstacles and enemies alike. Her ultimate creates a barrier that bounces enemies away, a safe space where Melissa and Muno can be together. Melissa is still a child at heart, playing with Muno when they're not fighting, dressing it in new outfits, telling it stories. She keeps a journal of their adventures, illustrated with crayon drawings, and she talks to her parents through it, hoping they can see her somehow. Muno cannot speak, but it communicates through gestures and warmth, and Melissa understands. Together, they are a family, a girl and her doll, facing a world that can be scary, but never lonely.
famousFor: Her doll Muno and zone control.

abilities.name: Scissors
abilities.description: Melissa attacks with her scissors, dealing damage.

abilities.name: Muno, Go!
abilities.description: She sends Muno to an area, damaging and slowing enemies.

abilities.name: Toy Factory
abilities.description: Passively, she gains attack speed. Active: She creates a zone that slows enemies.

ultimate: Melissa creates a barrier around her that bounces enemies away and prevents them from entering.
aura: Sparkles and toy sounds.
personality: Playful, lonely, and fiercely protective of Muno. She treats the doll as her only friend.

name: Floryn
lore: Floryn was born in a field of moonflowers, her tiny wings still wet with dew, and the first thing she saw was a shooting star. The fairies of the meadow took her in, raising her on nectar and starlight, teaching her the ancient art of dreamweaving. Floryn could enter the dreams of sleeping creatures, planting seeds of hope or calming nightmares. She was beloved by all, her laughter like wind chimes, her touch warm as sunlight. But when a plague of nightmares swept through the land, corrupting dreams into terrors, Floryn ventured into the nightmare realm to confront the source. She found a creature of pure fear, a being that fed on despair, and in the battle that followed, she sacrificed her own dreams to seal it away. Now Floryn can no longer dream—when she sleeps, there is only darkness—but she can still heal the dreams of others. Her ultimate heals all allies globally, a wave of dream energy that soothes even the most troubled sleep. Floryn is gentle and kind, always offering a soft word to the weary, but she carries the weight of her lost dreams in her heart. She keeps a journal of the dreams she has saved, describing them in vivid detail, hoping that by writing them down, she can experience them vicariously. In quiet moments, she visits sleeping children, watching their faces as they dream, and she whispers blessings over them. Floryn knows that she can never dream again, but she has learned that protecting the dreams of others is its own kind of magic, and that sometimes, the greatest gift is the one you give away.
famousFor: Her global heal and purify.

abilities.name: Dream Bloom
abilities.description: Floryn throws a flower that damages and slows enemies.

abilities.name: Sweet Scent
abilities.description: She heals an ally and cleanses crowd control.

abilities.name: Peaceful Slumber
abilities.description: Passively, she gains magic power. Active: She puts an enemy to sleep.

ultimate: Floryn heals all allies globally and removes crowd control effects from them.
aura: Floating flower petals.
personality: Gentle, caring, and optimistic. She believes in the power of dreams and kindness.

name: Julian
lore: Julian was ten years old when he first picked up a sword, and by eleven, he had defeated every adult in his village. By twelve, he had mastered three schools of magic, and by thirteen, he had been accepted into the most prestigious academy in the land. The masters called him a prodigy, a genius, the future of magic. Julian believed them. He trained obsessively, pushing himself beyond any limit, driven by a need to be the best. But the pressure began to crack him—he started hearing voices, fragments of spells echoing in his mind, and his magic became unstable, unpredictable. During a demonstration for visiting dignitaries, his control slipped, and a spell erupted, injuring several people. The academy expelled him, and Julian found himself alone, his identity shattered. He wandered for years, trying to understand what was happening to him. He discovered that his magic was evolving, his skills changing based on how he used them, as if his very essence was rewriting itself. His ultimate shifts based on his previous casts, offering different effects—a reflection of his fractured, ever‑changing self.
